title: Pouteria eerwah
taxonomy: Pouteria eerwah is classified within the family Sapotaceae. This family consists of over 900 species of tropical and subtropical plants. Pouteria is a genus within Sapotaceae, characterized by its deciduous or evergreen trees and shrubs. The species within this genus are typically found in Australia and other parts of the world with warm climates. The classification of Pouteria eerwah as a member of the Sapotaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within this group.
uses: Pouteria eerwah, a species of tree native to Australia, has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties. The tree's bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bark is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin irritations. intro: Pouteria eerwah, also known as the 'bush cherry' or 'native cherry', is a native Australian plant species belonging to the family Sapotaceae.
